The bathroom needs one more towel for guests to put on the floor to avoid slipping. There's an ironing board, but no iron.
Booking says there is tea and coffee making facilities but there wasn't. Breakfast is available for an extra cost but you should know its in another building. Reception desk is hard to find as it has another name which is confusing and I was told parking was full here but when I arrived the receptionist asked if we wanted to use the parking.
Overall, it's acceptable for a one-night stay, but more than that I'm not sure. Photos here are extremely staged.

title: Good option for families for 1 night stay during road trips

neg_text: No private parking (although I reserved the parking, that reservation didn't mean anything for reception staff and she simply decided to give that parking slot to another hotel guest), no shower gel in both bathrooms, distance to the hotel (for breakfast) was a bit too far (15min walk), too close to the train station (hence couldn't sleep in the morning due to trains' noise), noise problem wouldn't bother in winter though (when there's no need to keep the windows open due to hot [+23C] weather), no room air conditioning (although could keep the windows open all the time).

hotel_name: Deluxe Apartments by The Railway Station Wroclaw - parking w cenie
title: Good
pos_text:
Location - very close (walking distance) to the main railway station in a modern apartment building.
Price—The Genius 3 level was a real bargain. It was almost like the price of a dinner in a restaurant (~35 EUR per apartment for 2).
Apartment was clean
Helpful staff
neg_text:
No iron and hair dryer, even though they were supposed to be standard accessories as per Booking.com page.
Paper-thin walls, so you could hear neighbours.
Lack of toiletries in the bathroom was a disappointment and a bad surprise in the morning. Was totally unprepared for this in case of a company-run apartment.
Check-in was a bit chaotic and complicated - I received a message via Booking.com to check-in online, as so I did. Only to be informed at the reception desk that they need to to it again anyway in person during check-in.
Same for the invoice - I received a request via Booking.com to provide company details for an invoice within 2 hours time otherwise they would be able to issue the invoice for me. That was confusing cause I did that during the booking process in the very beginning. In the end it turned out that this is an automated bot message they send to everyone and they handed me the paper invoice during check-in. I felt blackmailed to be honest. The check-in and invoice issues are an obvious process problem, but the staff handled it professionally in the end.

hotel_name: Deluxe Apartments by The Railway Station Wroclaw - parking w cenie
title: The photos on Booking are too old and don't illustrate the actual condition of the place.
pos_text: The location and the cleaner's work are great, but the rest is disappointing.
neg_text:
The condition of the apartments is terrible, unlike in the photos: the walls are dirty, and all the lights are full of dead insects, in one of the bedrooms the door is simply missing (off its hinges), both toilets do not close (in one of them even the door itself does not close, in the second - only the lock). Also, there was no TV or hair dryer in the room, and the set of dishes was not complete for 5 adults.

The heating in the duplex is completely uncontrolled: on the ground floor, it cannot be turned on; in the lower part of the room, it is cold; in the upper part, it cannot be turned off, which makes it very stuffy and you have to open the windows constantly.

The shower stall is leaking, one of the sinks is missing a stopper, and instead of it, there is a bottle cap lying in the drain. The bed linen and towels look like they have been used for years and totally differ from the photos - they seem the cheapest.

In the photos, you can find what this apartment COULD look like, but it feels like somebody tried to reduce all the possible comfort for the economy.

hotel_name: Deluxe Apartments by The Railway Station Wroclaw - parking w cenie
title: Shocking experience – unsafe, unclean, and completely different from the photos.
pos_text: The only positive thing was the location – very central
neg_text:
Almost everything else was a terrible experience. The apartment looked nothing like the photos shown on Booking. While it was mentioned that room design might differ, what we received was not even close — the furniture was very old, dirty, damaged, and some items were missing completely. For example, there was no wardrobe in the bedroom and no coffee table in the living room, although both were listed or shown in the booking photos. Broken curtains, and completely unsafe equipment. I injured my hand on a sharp, exposed part of the bathroom door handle.
The apartment reeked of smoke, even though it was listed as non-smoking. My child had an asthma attack because of it. The toilet was filthy, including the seat and inside the bowl – it felt unsafe to use and made us worry about possible infections.
The bedding was ripped and stained, with not enough prepared for all guests. The dishes were only enough for 3 people, although the booking was for 4.
When we complained, the response was slow and inadequate. They only replaced the torn sheet and brought one more blanket and towel – again, not enough for everyone. They refused to change the apartment or take real action to fix the problems. Overall, it was extremely disappointing and unacceptable. It’s a pity I can only upload four photos — I wish I could share them all so you could truly see how terrible the conditions were and how serious and large the injury to my hand was.

hotel_name: Deluxe Apartments by The Railway Station Wroclaw - parking w cenie
title: I would not recommend it!
pos_text: The building is nice, but it is the only thing I can say.
neg_text:
I booked for 2 adults with 2 kids and parking (additional fee). This is what we received:
1. We have received a terrible room with intense smell of smoke and room was for 3 people instead of 4. Bed linens looked terrible.
2. After complain, we have received another appartment. It was much better; however:
a. The additional bed linens we received in old ikea bag and it did not look fresh.
b. Beds are not comfortable at all, it was double bed which makes sound on every movement and leather soda bed (no additional matress to cover it).
c. It was crazy noisy during all time, esp. night time, party sounds, workers, garbage truck, etc.
d. No hairdryer in the bathroom! Bathroom facilieties needs to be repaired (shower wall even had mould, sink looked broken). There were wahing machine in bathroom, but we did not use it.
c. Kitchen - looked ok, but we did not use anything.
d. TV only polish channels.
e. We paid additionally for parking place, we left for few hours and our parking place was taken or given to somebody else. It is really disapponting, especially when you travel with kids and the one of the criteria for selection is proper parking. At the end, we have received another parking place, but it takes additional calls and time!

Never ever again I will book apparthotel and I do not recommend it for any family with kids. For the same price on that night was a nice hotel in city centre and I believe it would be much better experience.

hotel_name: Deluxe Apartments by The Railway Station Wroclaw - parking w cenie
title: Don't be fooled by the photos!
pos_text: The location was central, near to the train station and town centre, which suited our needs as we only visited Wroclaw for a daytime music concert and short stroll through the old town.
neg_text: The first room we were given was shocking. It didn't resemble the photos at all! The room was empty, with little to no furniture, scuffs and damage to walls and the remaining furniture. After complaining to the receptionist, they reluctantly gave us a different room. However, it was only a minor improvement from the previous room and felt more like a one night stop over for people going out to party. We were kept awake most of the night due to the neighbours having a full night of very loud 'passion'. Also, the apartment felt grim and dirty, leaving us all sleeping fully clothed. Very disappointed. Wouldn't recommend.

hotel_name: Deluxe Apartments by The Railway Station Wroclaw - parking w cenie
title: Excellent value, extremely comfortable and wish I had spent my whole trip here!
pos_text: Excellent all round. The location was fantastic for access to the bus and rail station - there’s also a small shop (zabka) opposite and budget about 3 minute walk away. The bed was super comfy and the apartment spotless. I really appreciated the range of equipment available - even a dishwasher and kitchen ware - and did my laundry there, which was very handy as I had limited time to visit a laundrette. The desk staff were incredibly helpful and kind and spoke excellent English. The apartment is beautiful and I’m so glad I could stay here for the night for such a reasonable price - not much more than a hostel!
neg_text: Although all equipment is available, some items like the clothing rack is only okay to pick up from the front desk. This is perfectly understandable but as these are quite large they are a little cumbersome to bring up to the apartment - might be worth just storing one of each in the apartments. I also struggled a little with the key code to get to the stairwell as this had settings only in Polish and was not as simple as the instructions suggested - but I got the hang of it with some help!

hotel_name: HP Park Plaza
title: Good location
pos_text: The room was a good size and beds were comfortable. We opted not to stay right in the centre because the reviews for pretty much all of the hotels there said that it was noisy at night, so this location was a good compromise - close enough to walk to all of the main attractions, but quieter. (We were on the street side so there was a bit of rumbling from the trams, but not a peep coming through the walls or door, so overall really good sound-proofing.) The terrace and restaurant have lovely views. Bottled water was provided each day and tea/coffee replenished, which was nice. Wroclaw is a fantastic city, so I will definitely be back! Don't miss the botanic gardens - absolutely gorgeous. :-)
neg_text: There was a really bad smell - like a sewer - in the hallways and lobby on all three days that we were there. They should install better drapes, because it was bright enough to perform surgery in the room from 5 a.m. onwards (my bad - one should always travel with a sleep mask!). It would have been nice to have more toiletries in the bathroom, like conditioner and lotion, instead of just soap and shower gel. Overall, it felt more like a 3-star hotel rather than 4-stars, but given the price that we paid, it was satisfactory.

hotel_name: Great Polonia The Granary La Suite Hotel
title: Good location; great for families
pos_text: I stayed on a Saturday night with my 6 year old. We’d booked an Executive Room but the hotel extremely kindly upgraded us to a duplex suite which was gorgeous and very comfortable indeed - thank you so much! The duplex suite has a comfortable bed and en suite complete with corner bath downstairs; upstairs a toilet, second bedroom and a living room area with mini kitchen (complete with a microwave!), dining table and well stocked and well priced minibar. This would be a great place for a family of four. The kitchen is sufficiently equipped for 4 people but is a bit short on glassware. The building being in an old granary is lovely and unique. We were fortunate enough to be in Wroclaw during their Dwarf Festival and there was so much going on in the park just one block from the hotel! It is close to everything but not at all noisy.
neg_text: Very minor points but…No wine glasses or corkscrew! We didn’t have it in the end but were told breakfast was 100PLN which felt a little steep.

hotel_name: Great Polonia The Granary La Suite Hotel
title: Lovely atmospheric place
pos_text:
We had a Garden Room - very spacious with a proper sitting area inside and a table & chairs outside.
The room itself is absolutely lovely with an arched brick ceiling.
Comfy bed and proper Grohe shower, fridge with mini bar, quiet AC.
Breakfast looked very good as we passed (we never have breakfast, we always go out for coffee & croissants!)
Staff were absolutely lovely & helped out when needed (eg with luggage storage and lost key cards).
neg_text: Really I don’t recognise many of the criticisms in other reviews. To be perfectly honest it is more of a 4 than 5 star. The TV needs upgrading and the garden isn’t beautiful! But we had a lovely room for the weekend, much more pleasant than the usual square box, for just over £100 a night.

title: 5* for this hotel is an overrating

neg_text: This hotel is rated 5* but, in my opinion, it doesn't make the cut. The luggage/wheelchair lift at the stairs down to reception wouldn't work and didn't look like any attempts to service it were made during our stay. When we lifted the blinds covering sloping parts of windows, it revealed a filthy glass, obviously the windows haven't been cleaned for months. The floor lamp in the room wouldn't work either. Such a things shouldn't happened in the hotel, which is pretending to 5* rating.

hotel_name: Great Polonia The Granary La Suite Hotel
title: Small details cost value
pos_text:
Spacious room (executive) with a lot of light and very wide bed, slept with husband and baby and everybody had space.
Liked the a bit separate living room/ tv area.
Location is ideal, close to mall, old town, very connected by buses, trams, but at the same time quiet and chill.
Extremely nice and welcoming staff.
neg_text:
During our time there, the elevator from front door to lobby didn't work, so we had to carry stroller with baby up and down the stairs. Also our requested (and approved) cot was not provided to us, but as we arrived late and baby was already very done for the day, we didn't go to ask for it either. Later ended up co-sleeping.
Also our TV didn't connect to wifi, so 2 nights out of 4 we could not use tv. As the hotel was fully booked, they couldn't relocate us either.
Room over all could use some refreshing.

title: The food and location was good, but the receptionist's behavior was unacceptable. Avoid if you can!
pos_text: The food was good, and the location was good, but the receptionist's behavior was unacceptable.
neg_text: The first impression was good. However, since the air conditioning in the room was loud, we asked to have it checked and went for a two-hour walk. Upon returning, the room was still very noisy. The receptionist, Mr. Szymon, rudely informed me that it was definitely my fault and pointed out that it was the ventilation, not the air conditioning. When I pointed out that he was talking to my wife and me while sitting back in his chair (we are twice his age), he remained in that position, twice refusing to give his name. We were eventually assigned a different room, but the unpleasant impression remained. The hotel was nice, the food was good, and the location was good, but the receptionist's behavior was unacceptable. This is absolutely not the standard of a five-star hotel.. Avoid this place if you can.

We were cold in the room and the air conditioning did not allow us to increase the temperature. We complained to the staff who were unable to change the temperature either. They offered to change our room but the temperature was the same every where. Clearly the problem was structural and the staff did not have a manual override for the centrally controlled system. In the end they gave us a free standing radiator which was ridiculous for a 5 star hotel but at least it did the job.
We were also very disappointed with the choice and quality of food in the restaurant. The schnitzel was so heavily seasoned with pepper that I only ate half mine and my husband couldn’t eat his at all.

hotel_name: Great Polonia The Granary La Suite Hotel
title: Level of poor quality 3 star hotel
pos_text: In general nothing
neg_text:
First of all, their Premium suite for 220 EUR a night, looks like a standard 3 star hotel room but dirty!
Upon arrival i was given a room which was not ready and was on the ground floor, needless to mention when i made reservation 3 months ago, it was shown that it will be top floor Premium suite. When i got my room, when entering the room i immediately noticed huge dirty spot on the carpet, and overall carpets were dirty and worn out. Glass surfaces (bathroom door) was with visible fingerprints. Bathroom floor was not clean, it was dusty, with hair and some white substance marks on it. Windows were very dirty. When i complained to receptionist and requested their manager to reach out to me, my request and messages in booking.com were totally ignored. Currently i have requested Booking.com to reconsider this hotel 5 star rating on their app, because hotel itself has the quality of poor 3 star rating hotel.
Moreover, one hour before breakfast ends i came to restaurant. Menu was depleted and only after asking to refresh it, it was done.
If You don’t want your Wroclaw visit experience to be ruined as it was for us and You want to avoid overpriced hotel, then i totally do not recommend this establishment!!!

hotel_name: Great Polonia The Granary La Suite Hotel
title: Just ok, it’s functional and conveniently located but wouldn’t rush back.
pos_text: It was conveniently located, the rooms were nice and large.
neg_text: It just felt lacking.. everything it was ok. practical and functional, the staff, the rooms, the bar etc. but it just felt like it was missing something. We didn’t feel particularly welcome, there were no pleasantries or warmth from the staff. The room we were given looked out onto a brick wall (our room was changed when we complained), the billiard room is basically an empty bedroom with two pool tables in it. Randomly for a hotel with no pool it has pictures of a lovely pool in the reception area. We asked about the massages, but when they mentioned it would be done in the hotel room, we declined. It just felt a bit weird. I’m not sure it feels right that it’s promoted as a hotel with ‘wellness’ facilities.

The garden room had no garden but a rough bit of weeds and no drinking water in the room. The desk staff did their best but the hotel appeared to be on its last legs . For what was supposed to be a 5 star hotel it was awful and overpriced

hotel_name: Great Polonia The Granary La Suite Hotel
title: Pleasant
neg_text: We arrived late as family of 6 to basically just spend the night in 2 apartments. We had to wait initially in the lobby because somehow one of the apartments was not booked for us at all, even though we were there at midday initially to leave our baggage. When we finally got to our bigger apartment for 2 adults and 2 children there was only one bed. We have been upgraded for free but had to wait another 30-40 min for another apartment to be ready. All in all, we arrived around 21:30, with kids, after a full day of walking and could eventually go to sleep around 23:00. I'm sure anyone with kids can appreciate that 😀

hotel_name: Great Polonia The Granary La Suite Hotel
title: Overall overpriced, but nice breakfast
pos_text: The a-la carte breakfast is great, fresh and hot. The staff are overall really friendly. The location is in the very city center, where everything interesting is within 20 min walk.
neg_text:
The extra parking charge. Somehow I missed that info in the booking, but the price was surprisingly high, and due to the location, there was no option to just leave the car in the street. I know, the city center = make money of each centimeter, that you own, but with this price for stay... Well, it's way too much.
Also, the fancy 45 degrees panoramic windows were a great hope, when I was booking. But in reality, they were fully covered in birds excrements, which didn't look quite good :)
Also, well, if you plan on having a vacation, mind that there's a school right next, so at 8, the kinds are gonna shout, and closed windows won't help.
Also, the carpet in our room, unfortunately, was once stained with something strong, so apparently they couldn't create it all away — I was not entirely fond of those old stains. Again, considering the price.
